title: Friday Abstinence
description: Traditional Catholics abstain from meat on ALL Fridays of the year, not only during Lent. This is the traditional discipline that was universally binding before 1966. Many traditional communities maintain this practice year-round as an act of penance in honor of Our Lord's Passion.

title: Wednesday Abstinence (Traditional)
description: In the older discipline, Wednesdays were also days of abstinence during Advent and Lent. Some traditional Catholics maintain Wednesday abstinence year-round as an additional act of penance.

title: Saturday Abstinence (Traditional)
description: Traditionally, Saturdays were days of partial abstinence in honor of Our Lady. Meat was permitted only at the principal meal. This practice has largely fallen into disuse but remains meritorious.
seasonalNote: In honor of the Blessed Virgin Mary

title: Ember Days
description: Four sets of three days (Wednesday, Friday, Saturday) occurring roughly at the start of each season. Days of fasting and partial abstinence. Traditionally a time to pray for the sanctification of the clergy and for priestly vocations.
seasonalNote: Advent, Lent, Pentecost week, September. Wednesday and Saturday: partial abstinence. Friday: full abstinence.

title: Vigil Fasts
description: Traditionally, the vigils (day before) of major feasts required fasting: Christmas, Pentecost, Assumption (Aug 14), All Saints (Oct 31), and others. These are days of fasting and abstinence to prepare spiritually for the feast.
seasonalNote: Dec 24, Pentecost Eve, Aug 14, Oct 31, and others

title: Morning Offering
description: Begin each day by offering all prayers, works, joys, and sufferings to the Sacred Heart of Jesus. This simple act consecrates your entire day and unites your actions with the Holy Sacrifice of the Mass being offered around the world.

title: The Angelus
description: Pray the Angelus three times daily: at 6 AM, noon, and 6 PM (when church bells traditionally ring). During Eastertide (Easter to Pentecost), the Regina Caeli replaces the Angelus. This devotion honors the Incarnation and keeps Our Lady's 'fiat' before our minds throughout the day.
seasonalNote: Replaced by Regina Caeli during Eastertide

title: Daily Rosary
description: The daily Rosary is the single most recommended devotion by the saints and by Our Lady herself at Fatima. Pray the mysteries assigned to today according to the traditional schedule. Even one decade prayed well is meritorious.

title: Examination of Conscience & Night Prayers
description: Before sleep, examine your conscience on the day's sins and failings. Pray an Act of Contrition. Commend your soul to God. This traditional practice prepares the soul daily and is essential for regular Confession.

title: Attend Holy Mass
description: Attendance at the Holy Sacrifice of the Mass on Sundays and Holy Days of Obligation. This is not optional — it is a grave obligation binding under pain of mortal sin. Arrive before the Introit and remain through the Last Gospel.

title: First Friday Devotion
description: Receive Holy Communion on nine consecutive First Fridays in reparation to the Sacred Heart of Jesus. Our Lord promised to St. Margaret Mary Alacoque that those who complete this devotion will receive the grace of final perseverance.

title: First Saturday Devotion
description: On five consecutive First Saturdays: go to Confession (within 8 days), receive Holy Communion, pray five decades of the Rosary, and keep Our Lady company for 15 minutes meditating on the mysteries. Requested by Our Lady of Fatima in reparation for sins against her Immaculate Heart.

title: Advent Practices
description: Advent is a penitential season (violet vestments, no Gloria). Traditional practices include: increased prayer and fasting, daily Rorate Mass attendance if possible, the Advent wreath, St. Andrew Christmas Novena (Nov 30–Dec 24), and preparing for Confession before Christmas.
seasonalNote: Four Sundays before Christmas through December 24

title: Lenten Practices
description: The forty days of penance before Easter. Traditional discipline requires fasting (one full meal, two smaller collations) on all weekdays. Abstinence from meat on all Fridays and Saturdays of Lent. Additional practices: Stations of the Cross on Fridays, increased almsgiving, giving up a pleasure, daily Mass attendance.

title: Rogation Days
description: The Major Rogation (April 25) and Minor Rogations (Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday before Ascension Thursday). Days of prayer and fasting to beg God's blessing on crops and protection from natural disasters. The Litany of the Saints is chanted in procession.
seasonalNote: April 25 (Major) and three days before Ascension (Minor)
